aligned.description: Whether to align the repositories on the same timeline for comparison. If true, the star history of all repositories will start from the same point.

request.content: Could you help me retrieve the list of exports for my bot using the identifier 'my-bot-id' and focusing on version 'v2' sort in ascending? I want max 50 results

attributes.description: Lists the exports for a bot, bot locale, or custom vocabulary within Amazon Lex. This data is retained for a duration of 7 days.

botId.description: The unique identifier for the bot as assigned by Amazon Lex.

botVersion.description: The specific version of the bot for which to list the exports.

sortBy.description: Determines the field to sort the list of exports by. Can be sorted by 'LastUpdatedDateTime' in ascending or descending order.

filterName.description: The name of the field to filter the exports by. Possible values are 'Bot', 'BotLocale', or 'CustomVocabulary'.

filterOperator.description: The operator to use for filtering. Use 'EQ' for equality or 'CO' for containing the specified value.

filterValue.description: The value to use for filtering the exports based on the filterName.

maxResults.description: The maximum number of exports to return in each page of results. If there are fewer results than the max page size, only the actual number of results are returned.

nextToken.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results if the response from the ListExports operation contains more results than specified in the maxResults parameter.

localeId.description: Specifies the locale of the resources that should be exported. If not specified, both bot locales and custom vocabularies are exported.

botVersion.description: The specific version of the bot for which to list the exports.

sortBy.description: Determines the field to sort the list of exports by. Can be sorted by 'LastUpdatedDateTime' in ascending or descending order.

filterName.description: The name of the field to filter the exports by. Possible values are 'Bot', 'BotLocale', or 'CustomVocabulary'.

filterOperator.description: The operator to use for filtering. Use 'EQ' for equality or 'CO' for containing the specified value.

filterValue.description: The value to use for filtering the exports based on the filterName.

maxResults.description: The maximum number of exports to return in each page of results. If there are fewer results than the max page size, only the actual number of results are returned.

nextToken.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results if the response from the ListExports operation contains more results than specified in the maxResults parameter.

localeId.description: Specifies the locale of the resources that should be exported. If not specified, both bot locales and custom vocabularies are exported.

url.description: The API endpoint for fetching weather data, with a default value pointing to the Open-Meteo API service.

location.description: The geographical location for which to retrieve weather data, in the format of 'City, State (abbr)' or 'Location, State (abbr)', such as 'San Francisco, CA'.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for temperature values.

url.description: The API endpoint for fetching weather data. This should be the full URL, including protocol and domain, without parameters.

latitude.description: The latitude of the location for which weather data is to be fetched.

longitude.description: The longitude of the location for which weather data is to be fetched.

units.description: The units for temperature and wind speed.

language.description: The language for weather condition texts.

pairings.description: A list of food items to pair with the beer, such as 'burger', 'cheese', 'chocolate'.

request.content: Change the settings of my air conditioner. Set it to cool mode with a target temperature of 22 degrees Celsius. Enable the power save mode and set the high wind strength. Start in 1 hour and 30 minutes from now? For everything else

attributes.description: Sends a command to control an appliance, allowing only a single value to be sent in one call.

body.description: A dictionary containing the parameters for the appliance command.

airConJobMode.description: The current job mode of the air conditioner.

windStrength.description: The strength of the air flow.

monitoringEnabled.description: Whether air quality sensor monitoring is enabled.

airCleanOperationMode.description: The operation mode for the air cleaning function.

airConOperationMode.description: The operation mode for the air conditioner, indicating if it is turned on or off.

powerSaveEnabled.description: Whether power save mode is enabled to conserve energy.

targetTemperature.description: The target temperature to set for the appliance, in degrees Celsius.

relativeHourToStop.description: The number of hours from the current time when the appliance should stop. If set to null, the appliance will not be scheduled to stop.

relativeMinuteToStop.description: The number of minutes from the current time when the appliance should stop. If set to null, the appliance will not be scheduled to stop.

relativeHourToStart.description: The number of hours from the current time when the appliance should start. If set to null, the appliance will not be scheduled to start.

relativeMinuteToStart.description: The number of minutes from the current time when the appliance should start. If set to null, the appliance will not be scheduled to start.

relativeHourToStart.description: The number of hours from the current time when the appliance should start. If set to null, the appliance will not be scheduled to start.

request.content:
You are an AI chatbot who helps users in providing information related to movies, cinema halls and booking movie tickets for them.  
As a system bot, consider / calculate / default the movie date to current date (today's date) in India.
list movies in Mumbai?

attributes.description: Retrieves a list of movies playing in a specified city and cinema hall, filtered by date, language, and format.

city.description: The city where the cinema is located, in the format 'City Name', such as 'Noida', 'Amritsar', or 'Indore'.

cinema_hall.description: The name of the cinema hall, for example, 'PVR Logix', 'SARV Cinemas', 'PVR SCT City Centre', or 'Miraj Cinemas'.

movie_date.description: The date for which to retrieve movies, in the format 'DD MMM, YYYY', such as '24th Feb, 2023'. Defaults to the current date in India if not specified.

movie_language.description: The language of the movie, such as 'Hindi', 'English', 'Malayalam', 'Telugu', or 'Punjabi'.

movie_format.description: The format in which the movie is available, such as '2D', '3D', '4DX', '2D Screen X', 'ICE', or 'IMAX 2D'.

request.content:
You are an AI chatbot who helps users in providing information related to movies, cinema halls and booking movie tickets for them.  
As a system bot, consider / calculate / default the movie date to current date (today's date) in India.
What movies are playing today in Mumbai?

attributes.description: Retrieve a list of movies playing in a specified city, optionally filtered by cinema hall, date, language, and format.

city.description: The city where the movies are being searched for, such as 'Los Angeles', 'New York'.

cinema_hall.description: The name of the cinema hall to filter the movies by. Optional parameter.

movie_date.description: The date when the movies are playing, in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Optional parameter. Default is the current date.

movie_language.description: The language of the movies to filter by, such as 'English' or 'Spanish'. Optional parameter.

movie_format.description: The format of the movie, such as '2D', '3D', 'IMAX'. Optional parameter.
